mechanism_narrative: A high and rising unemployment rate (UNRATE) signals significant slack in the economy, depressing consumer demand and corporate earnings expectations, which leads to negative forward returns for the S&P 500.
failure_condition: The S&P 500 produces positive 60-day returns in more than 50% of instances when the trigger condition is met.

mechanism_narrative: With the federal funds rate near the zero lower bound, the environment of maximum monetary accommodation provides strong support for equity valuations through low discount rates. This effect persists as long as rates remain low.
failure_condition: The average 20-day forward return when FEDFUNDS is below 0.5% is not statistically higher than when it is above 0.5% (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A concurrent decline in housing starts (HOUST) and retail sales (RSAFS) signals a broad-based contraction in consumer spending on both durable goods and services, leading to lower corporate revenues and negative equity returns.
failure_condition: The hit rate of the signal (predicting negative 60-day returns) does not exceed 55% over a full business cycle.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained positive trend in industrial production (INDPRO) reflects a strengthening supply side of the economy, indicating rising corporate activity and profitability, which drives positive equity market performance.
failure_condition: The rolling 36-month rank correlation between 3-month changes in INDPRO and 20-day forward S&P 500 returns is consistently below 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: The combination of high unemployment (UNRATE) and low inflation (CPIAUCSL) gives the central bank maximum flexibility to maintain accommodative policy, suppressing bond yields and supporting higher equity multiples.
failure_condition: S&P 500 returns are not significantly positive (one-sided t-test p > 0.05) during periods when the trigger condition is met.

mechanism_narrative: Strong month-over-month growth in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) is a primary, high-frequency indicator of economic health, boosting investor sentiment and leading to positive short-term equity returns around the release date.
failure_condition: The average 5-day forward return following a trigger event is not statistically distinguishable from the average return on non-trigger days.

mechanism_narrative: A contraction in real GDP (GDPC1) is the canonical signal of a recession. This confirmation of broad economic weakness leads to downward revisions of earnings forecasts and negative pressure on the S&P 500.
failure_condition: The S&P 500 posts positive 60-day returns in more than 50% of the quarters following a negative real GDP print.

mechanism_narrative: An acceleration in the Consumer Price Index (CPIAUCSL) signals rising inflation, which erodes corporate profit margins and increases the probability of future monetary tightening, both of which are negative for equity valuations.
failure_condition: The information coefficient of the signal remains below 0 for a rolling 3-year period, indicating no predictive power.
